description: Test hypotheses about population variances using the chi-square test for a single variance and the F-test for the ratio of two independent variances

fields.name: sample_1
fields.label: Observations or summary stats (s^2, n) for sample 1

single_variance_test.distribution: Chi-square with n-1 degrees of freedom
single_variance_test.two_sided_rule: Reject H0 if chi_square < chi_{1-alpha/2, n-1} OR chi_square > chi_{alpha/2, n-1}

two_variance_ratio_test.statistic: F = s_1^2 / s_2^2 (with s_1^2 >= s_2^2 by convention)
two_variance_ratio_test.distribution: F-distribution with (n_1 - 1, n_2 - 1) degrees of freedom
two_variance_ratio_test.assumptions: Samples from normally distributed populations; independent samples
two_variance_ratio_test.convention: Place larger sample variance in numerator to ensure F >= 1; use right-tail critical value

interpretation.chi_square_sensitivity: Chi-square test is sensitive to departures from normality
interpretation.f_test_sensitivity: F-test is also sensitive to non-normality; Levene's test is a robust alternative
interpretation.non_symmetric_distributions: Both chi-square and F are right-skewed; two-sided tests use asymmetric critical values

applications.volatility_regime_change: Test whether volatility in period 2 differs from period 1 (F-test)
applications.risk_model_validation: Chi-square test of whether realised variance equals model-implied variance
applications.manager_risk_control: Test whether fund stdev exceeds stated mandate
applications.strategy_comparison: F-test for equal risk between two trading strategies

validation.variance_positive: Sample variances must be strictly positive
validation.normality_check: Warn if data fail normality tests — test results unreliable

run_chi_square_test.description: Chi-square test for a single variance

run_f_test.description: F-test for ratio of two variances

insufficient_data.description: Sample data missing or sample size too small

errors.message: Scenario must be single_variance or two_variance_ratio
Each sample requires at least 2 observations

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply the chi-square variance test to non-normal populations
anti_patterns.why: Chi-square test assumes normality; applying it to skewed or heavy-tailed data (e.g., financial returns) yields unreliable p-values. Use Levene's or bootstrap alternatives

anti_patterns.rule: Do not invert the F-ratio after computing if F < 1 and quote a left-tail p-value
anti_patterns.why: Convention is larger-variance numerator; manually inverting without recomputing critical values introduces sign and df confusion

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use the F-test when samples are paired or dependent
anti_patterns.why: F-test assumes independent samples; paired variance comparisons require Pitman-Morgan or bootstrap methods

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use the F-test as a preliminary check before the pooled t-test for equal variances
anti_patterns.why: Pretest bias: running the F-test and conditionally switching to Welch's t-test distorts overall Type I error. Use Welch's t-test unconditionally

goals.description: Test hypotheses about population variances using the chi-square test for a single variance and the F-test for the ratio of two independent variances

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details

chi_square_example.decision: Fail to reject H0 at alpha = 0.05 two-sided

f_test_example.decision: Reject H0 of equal variances at alpha = 0.05

related.reason: Chi-square tests are closely related variance-based hypothesis tests applied to categorical distributions
